referenced_message.content: So I’m out of luck with using Evadus?

content: So I’m out of luck with using Evadus?

Ok so I did some research, what I found is pretty interesting.

This is actually not a bug in the source code, instead it has to do with how your AMD CPU's features,

So AMD has a feature called Simultaneous Multithreading (SMT) that they use, instead of Intel's signature "Hyper-Threading"

So basically what happening in your example is that SMT is splitting one core into 2 virtualized cpu's, hence why it says 2 sockets (2 separate cpus), and only reporting 2 cores in total, even though there are 4 cores across both cpu's.

So this is actually not a bug, this is expected behaviour from SMT.

It is just shit unlucky that Guardian does not take into account SMT (what else do you expect from a lack-lester anti-cheat company), otherwise this would not be a problem, since you do technically have 4 cores available

What guardian is essentially seeing is this:

1 cpu with 2 cores, which is kinda correct, since that is what the BIOS exposed on startup, however with SMT now it can virtualize a second CPU
